title: Chromatin Endogenous Cleavage and High-Throughput Sequencing (ChEC-seq) in S. cerevisiae
author: Gabe Zentner
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgthjwj6

steps.title: In the morning, dilute the overnight...
steps.instruction: In the morning, dilute the overnight culture to OD600 = 0.2-0.3 in 50 mL YPD or SC medium in a 300 mL flask. Grow 50 mL culture at 30°C until OD600 = 0.5-0.7.

steps.title: The day before the experiment, inoculate...
steps.instruction: The day before the experiment, inoculate 3 mL YPD or SC medium with a single colony. Grow overnight at 30°C.

steps.title: Harvest cells in a 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Harvest cells in a 50 mL conical tube at 1,500 x g for 1 min.

steps.title: Wash cells 3 x 1 mL Buffer A
steps.instruction: Wash cells 3 x 1 mL Buffer A. Transfer cells to a 1.5 mL tube with the first wash and spin as above between washes.

steps.title: Permeabilize cells
steps.instruction: Permeabilize cells. Resuspend pellet in 600 µL Buffer A + 0.1% digitonin (add 30 µL 2% digitonin in DMSO to 570 µL Buffer A) and incubate at 30°C for 5 min. Remove 100 µL as zero timepoint prior to step 6.

steps.title: Extract nucleic acids
steps.instruction: Extract nucleic acids. Add 200 µL phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol, mix well, and spin at max. speed for 5 min in a microfuge. Transfer aqueous phases to new tubes, add 2 µL (10 µg) linear acrylamide and 500 µL 100% ethanol, mix, and precipitate at -80°C for ≥30 min.

steps.title: Add 2 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K. Digest protein at 55°C for 20 min.

steps.title: Spin at max speed and 4°C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed and 4°C for 10 min.

steps.title: Wash pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol and aspirate ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol and aspirate ethanol.

steps.title: Briefly air-dry pellets and resuspend in...
steps.instruction: Briefly air-dry pellets and resuspend in 29 µL Qiagen EB or comparable buffer + 1 µL 10 mg/mL RNase A and incubate at 37°C for 10 min.

steps.title: Run 5 µL RNase-treated DNA on a 1
steps.instruction: Run 5 µL RNase-treated DNA on a 1.5% agarose gel to check DNA fragmentation if desired.

steps.title: Dilute RNase-treated DNA to 200 µL...
steps.instruction: Dilute RNase-treated DNA to 200 µL with Qiagen EB or comparable buffer.

steps.title: Add 160 µL Ampure beads (0
steps.instruction: Add 160 µL Ampure beads (0.8:1 beads:sample ratio) and pipet up and down 10X to mix. Incubate at room temperature for 5 min.

steps.title: Collect beads on magnetic rack for 2 min
steps.instruction: Collect beads on magnetic rack for 2 min.

steps.title: Remove the supernantant (~400 µL) to...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernantant (~400 µL) to a new tube containing 16 µL 5 M NaCl (~200 mM final).

steps.title: Extract DNA from the unbound fraction
steps.instruction: Extract DNA from the unbound fraction. Add 400 µL phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol, mix well, and spin at max. speed for 5 min in a microfuge.

steps.title: Transfer aqueous phases to new tubes,...
steps.instruction: Transfer aqueous phases to new tubes, add 2 µL (10 µg) linear acrylamide and 1 mL 100% ethanol, mix, and precipitate at -80C for ≥30 min.

steps.title: Spin at max speed and 4°C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed and 4°C for 10 min.

steps.title: Wash pellets with 1 mL 75%...
steps.instruction: Wash pellets with 1 mL 75% ethanol and remove ethanol with vacuum.

steps.title: Briefly air-dry pellets and resuspend in...
steps.instruction: Briefly air-dry pellets and resuspend in 25 µL Qiagen EB or comparable buffer. Recovered DNA can be quantified by Qubit and the size distribution analyzed via TapeStation using a high-sensitivity tape. Sequencing libraries can be prepared using any standard ChIP-seq-style method.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.1 µL 1 M CaCl 2 (~2 mM final), mix, and incubate at 30°C. A recent study mapping the genome-wide distribution of components of the SAGA and TFIID coactivator complexes (PMID 31913117) with ChEC used a 10-fold lower calcium concentration with a 5 min digestion time in order to minimize background genomic digestion and overdigestion of target sites.

steps.title: At each desired time point, remove...
steps.instruction: At each desired time point, remove a 100 µL aliquot of the digest to a tube containing 90 µL stop solution and 10 µL 10% SDS and vortex to mix. We have performed multiple time course ChEC experiments for both MNase-tagged factors and free MNase in distinct genetic backgrounds. For free MNase, we have observed that relatively long digestion times (≥ 2.5 min) yield cleavage of promoter nucleosome-depleted regions and nucleosome linkers due to their accessibility. It is therefore difficult to determine if enrichment of a particular factor is specific at these later time points. However, free MNase signal is quite low at short time points, allowing clear distinction between MNase-tagged factor and free MNase signal. We therefore recommend that, once an initial time course to determine the digestion kinetics of the factor under study and its corresponding free MNase control is performed, short time points be used for further experiments. We have found that a ≤ 1 min digestion is sufficient for all tested factors using the digestion conditions provided here. Free MNase signal increases substantially over time. Heatmaps of CPM-normalized free MNase signal across a 40-fold range of digestion times in two distinct genetic backgrounds with free MNase expression driven by two different promoters. Heatmaps are centered around the TSSs of 4,857 genes encoding verified ORFs. Each heatmap is independently sorted in descending order by the average signal for each 2 kb window.

steps.instruction: data are also displayed for comparison. The following samples were combined following alignment with SAMtools merge :
